prompt: Create an ultra-realistic museum-quality graphite pencil sketch portrait on warm off-white textured drawing paper. The subject is a beautiful young South Asian woman shown from the chest up in a three-quarter view, looking downward with a soft closed-mouth smile while holding a single fully bloomed rose close to her nose. She has long dark hair gathered into a loose messy bun with wispy strands around her face, delicate facial features, and small stud earrings. She wears black rectangular-round eyeglasses with warm amber-tinted lenses as the only subtle color accent, plus a formal dark blazer, white collared shirt, and black tie. Her left hand gently holds the rose stem near her chest; the flower is large and detailed, with exactly 1 fully bloomed rose head, 1 long stem, and 6 visible leaves. Render the rose petals, leaves, fingers, hair strands, glasses, blazer, shirt collar, and tie with highly detailed pencil shading, crosshatching, smudged graphite gradients, fine linework, and realistic paper grain. Use a soft natural composition with the rose on the left side of the face, the woman centered slightly right, and faint loose sketch construction strokes fading into the background. Keep the image monochrome graphite except for the amber glasses lenses, avoid text, avoid watermark, and maintain an elegant romantic fine-art portrait mood.
